name: Alvin Ailey
abstract: Alvin Ailey Jr. (January 5, 1931 – December 1, 1989) was an American dancer, director, choreographer, and activist.

about_quotes.speaker: Arturo O'Farrill
about_quotes.evidence: I started working with dance companies with Ballet Hispanico, Malpaso, Alvin Ailey. I just scored — I didn’t score, I was commissioned by the San Francisco Ballet Company to write a full ballet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Arturo O'Farrill interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 6, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: George Russell
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I guess the one that always comes to mind immediately is Alvin Ailey and also Arthur Mitchell. [INTERVIEWEE] This one man came from the Alvin Ailey . . .
about_quotes.doc_title: George Russell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mary Lou Williams
about_quotes.evidence: "I had to train him. I said, 'Now I've been away a long time... So I told him some things to do and he caught on fast on his own.'"

about_quotes.speaker: Abbey Lincoln
about_quotes.evidence: Alvin Ailey finally directed it. ... Alvin helped. He came to help me. I mean, he was there and he directed the piece
about_quotes.doc_title: Abbey Lincoln — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Houston Person Part 2
about_quotes.evidence: And I don’t mean swing dancing, I mean with the ballet or Alvin Ailey, they do a lot of things with jazz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Houston Person Part 2 interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 2, 1998 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Whaley
about_quotes.evidence: Alvin Ailey, that's it. He had a girl -- tall, thin, black gal.
